title: Track external evidence provenance in graph deltas
problem: The 16-paper AG95 run produced accepted deltas whose notes say full arXiv PDFs were consulted, while validation still checks quotes against the local markdown excerpt/source only. This creates non-verbatim evidence warnings and makes it unclear which source supports a node or edge.
proposed_change: Add a minimal source/provenance field for evidence entries or delta-level consulted_sources, and teach validation/reporting to distinguish local-source quote misses from explicitly declared external-source evidence.
acceptance_criteria: A delta can declare external consulted sources; validation reports quote misses by source scope; run audit summarizes external-source usage; prompts instruct Codex to avoid undeclared external sources or cite them explicitly.

observations.context: ag95-diverse-003-16p: mr4701883 notes that full text for omitted sections was consulted from https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.01269 and contributed 78 evidence quote warnings; mr4792069 notes https://arxiv.org/pdf/2209.02568 and contributed 17 warnings. The current validator reports all as local quote misses, not source-scope provenance issues.
